reasoning: The probability of the US successfully acquiring or formally announcing the acquisition of Greenland by June 30, 2025, is practically zero. While the Trump administration, including VP JD Vance, has aggressively pursued the idea—even prompting a recent diplomatic trip to Greenland—the response from both Greenlandic and Danish leadership has been an absolute and unequivocal rejection. A transfer of sovereignty would require a complex, protracted treaty negotiation process and the mutual agreement of all parties, both of which are entirely absent and impossible to orchestrate in the remaining two and a half months. Currently, US legislative efforts (like the proposed "Red, White, and Blueland Act") are merely aimed at authorizing the Executive Branch to *begin* negotiations. Without any agreement or legal mechanism to forcibly annex the territory, there is no credible pathway to a YES resolution before the deadline.
